item.title: Fragments: Trailer Park Drama
item.description:
When I was 5 or 6, my family briefly lived in a trailer park in southern New Mexico. I don’t remember why we were living there. Probably to run away from something or someone, usually bill collectors or repo men. It was easier to do that back then.

I was outside playing with the neighborhood kids. We ran around and raised hell. At some point we rested at one of those big, wooden picnic tables at the center of the courtyard. We talked for a while and began busting each other’s balls. One of the kids took it a little too far and began insulting the others in a cruel way. He turned his attention toward me, called me names, made fun of my family. He jumped on top of the table and put his feet in my face. I couldn’t take the abuse anymore, so I grabbed his ankles and yanked as hard as I could. I guess I hurt him badly because he started crying, tears ran down his face; his cheeks turned bright red. The door to a nearby trailer swung open, and a big, balding man came storming over to us. It was the kid’s dad. The dad grabbed his wailing son and yelled at me to go home.

item.title: Proem
item.description:
I will be honest here: I set up this blog because I was curious about how to set up a static blog using GitHub as a host. This came to fruition out of some technical desire. Beyond that, there is no plan going forward.

The name Veblenia is derived from Veblenian, i.e. someone who adheres, in whole or in part, to the institutional economics of Thorstein Veblen. He was a fascinating character, and it would be well worth your time read up on him.

item.title: About
item.description:
About Veblenia

I haven’t really decided yet. When I do come up with one, I will put it here and pretend that was the idea all along. New visitors will never know.

About Cody

My name is Cody. I live in rural, southern New Mexico. I have three dogs and am doing one of those late-career pivots to IT. It’s going great. Big thumbs up emoji. I like tinkering with computers and my Emacs configuration. I don’t do much programming, but when I do, it’s usually some Python or Bash script to solve a one-off problem.
